chapter_summary: Chapter 10 begins a new section of Proverbs — Solomon's collection of short, crisp wisdom sayings. Each verse is like a little two-part picture: it shows a righteous person and a wicked person side by side, and lets you see the difference. Wise versus foolish. Hard-working versus lazy. Kind words versus hurtful words. Honest versus dishonest. God blesses the honest, the diligent, and the kind. The chapter is a gallery of contrasts, and every contrast is an invitation: which one do you want to be?
main_lesson: God blesses the honest, hard-working, and kind — but foolish and lazy choices lead to trouble.

memory_verse.kjv: Hatred stirreth up strifes: but love covereth all sins.
memory_verse.little_bible: Hate causes fights to grow bigger. But love covers over wrongs and makes things better!

parent_guide: Proverbs 10 opens the 'Proverbs of Solomon' section (chapters 10-22:16) which is structurally different from chapters 1-9. These are individual proverbs — pithy two-line contrasts rather than extended poems. Each proverb stands largely on its own. The antithetic parallelism ('wise/foolish', 'righteous/wicked') is a teaching device: by holding opposites together, wisdom is defined not abstractly but by comparison. For children, the proverb-pair format is actually very accessible — they can understand contrasts easily. Invite children to predict the second line after hearing the first.
application_for_children: This week, choose one proverb from chapter 10 to memorise. Every morning, say it out loud. At the end of the day, think: did I live the first part (the wise, righteous part) or the second part today?

verses.kjv: The proverbs of Solomon. A wise son maketh a glad father: but a foolish son is the heaviness of his mother.
verses.little_bible: A child who grows in wisdom makes their parents truly happy! But a child who keeps making foolish choices brings sadness to their family.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: A wise child makes parents glad.
verses.meaning: Wisdom is not just personal — it has a relational impact. Children who pursue wisdom bring joy to their parents; foolishness brings grief.
verses.memory_phrase: Wisdom gladdens your family.
verses.prayer: God, help me be a child who brings gladness to my family by growing in wisdom every day.
verses.discussion_question: What is one thing you can do this week that would make your parents glad?
verses.family_discussion: How do we express genuine gladness and delight when our children make wise choices — not just correct them when they are foolish?
verses.do_it_today: Do one thing today that you know will make your parent or carer glad — not for a reward, but because you love them.
verses.illustration_prompt: A joyful parent hugging a smiling child warmly, golden light around them, a warm family scene, watercolour style

verses.kjv: Treasures of wickedness profit nothing: but righteousness delivereth from death.
verses.little_bible: Things gained through cheating or wrongdoing help no one in the end. But doing what is right before God delivers you from real harm.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Wrongly gained treasure profits nothing. Righteousness saves.
verses.meaning: Wealth gained through wickedness is ultimately worthless — it cannot save from what truly matters. But righteousness has real, lasting deliverance.
verses.memory_phrase: Righteousness saves. Wickedness profits nothing.
verses.prayer: God, help me never want to gain things the wrong way. Help me trust that doing right is always better.
verses.discussion_question: If someone got a prize by cheating, would it really feel like a win? Why or why not?
verses.family_discussion: How do we help our children value honest gain over easy gain — in school, in games, and eventually in work?
verses.do_it_today: Think of one time you could take a shortcut that would not be honest. Choose the honest way instead.
verses.illustration_prompt: Two treasure chests: one full of dark coins that crumble into dust, one radiating a righteous warm golden light, watercolour comparison illustration

verses.kjv: The LORD will not suffer the soul of the righteous to famish: but he casteth away the substance of the wicked.
verses.little_bible: God will not let the righteous person go hungry — He takes care of them! But the wicked person's gains will not last.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: God takes care of righteous people.
verses.meaning: God is actively involved in providing for those who walk in righteousness. This is not a promise of wealth, but of God's faithful provision for those who trust Him.
verses.memory_phrase: God takes care of the righteous.
verses.prayer: God, thank You for taking care of me. Help me trust You for everything I need.
verses.discussion_question: Has God ever taken care of you when you were worried? What happened?
verses.family_discussion: How do we cultivate a trusting, grateful family culture — where we acknowledge God's provision rather than taking it for granted?
verses.do_it_today: Name three ways God has provided for your family this week. Thank Him specifically for each one.
verses.illustration_prompt: A child eating at a simple but abundant table, a gentle divine light blessing the meal, warm home watercolour scene

verses.kjv: He becometh poor that dealeth with a slack hand: but the hand of the diligent maketh rich.
verses.little_bible: A lazy person who puts in little effort ends up with little. But the person who works hard and diligently will see their efforts grow!
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Hard work brings good results. Lazy hands bring poverty.
verses.meaning: Diligence — focused, committed effort — produces results. Slackness (laziness, half-effort) produces poverty. The principle applies to every area of life.
verses.memory_phrase: Diligent hands bring good results.
verses.prayer: God, help me work with diligence — not half-effort, but full effort — in everything I do for Your glory.
verses.discussion_question: What is something you worked really hard at? What happened because of your effort?
verses.family_discussion: How do we cultivate a healthy work ethic in our children — not driven by anxiety or perfectionism, but by the wisdom of diligence?
verses.do_it_today: Pick one task today and do it with your very best effort — not just good enough, but excellent. See how it feels.
verses.illustration_prompt: Two scenes: one child working diligently with focused hands, a fruit tree growing beside them; another child sitting lazily with an empty basket, watercolour comparison

verses.kjv: He that gathereth in summer is a wise son: but he that sleepeth in harvest is a son that causeth shame.
verses.little_bible: The child who works when the work needs to be done is wise — they gather when the harvest is ready! But sleeping through the important moment brings shame.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Work when the time is right — do not miss the moment.
verses.meaning: Timing matters in wisdom. A harvest that is not gathered is wasted. Wisdom involves not just knowing what to do but when to do it — seizing the right moment.
verses.memory_phrase: Do the right thing at the right time.
verses.prayer: God, help me not miss the important moments. Give me wisdom to work and act when the time is right.
verses.discussion_question: Can you think of something that had a deadline — where you had to do it at the right time? What happened?
verses.family_discussion: How do we teach children the wisdom of timing — doing homework before playing, preparing before the event, acting when the moment is right?
verses.do_it_today: Do one thing today that you have been putting off. Don't wait — the harvest time is now.
verses.illustration_prompt: A child gathering golden grain in a sunny field while another child naps under a tree, the sun setting in the background, warm harvest watercolour

verses.kjv: Blessings are upon the head of the just: but violence covereth the mouth of the wicked.
verses.little_bible: The person who lives rightly has blessings raining down on their head! But the wicked person's own harmful words cover and ruin them.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Blessings rest on the head of the just.
verses.meaning: Righteousness attracts blessing — it sits upon the just person like a crown. But wickedness ultimately covers its own practitioners in the harm it causes.
verses.memory_phrase: Righteousness is crowned with blessings.
verses.prayer: God, help me live rightly so Your blessings can rest on my life. Keep my mouth from harmful words.
verses.discussion_question: What does it mean to have blessings 'on your head'? What blessings do you think God wants to pour on you?
verses.family_discussion: How do we speak blessings over our children — both in prayer and in words — affirming righteousness and building their identity in goodness?
verses.do_it_today: Place your hand on the head of each family member today and say a simple blessing over them.
verses.illustration_prompt: A smiling child with a gentle golden crown of light on their head, blessings falling like rain around them, warm summer watercolour

verses.kjv: The memory of the just is blessed: but the name of the wicked shall rot.
verses.little_bible: When a righteous person is remembered, their memory is a blessing to everyone who knew them. But the wicked person is forgotten — or remembered badly.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: A righteous person is remembered with blessing.
verses.meaning: Legacy and memory are the fruits of character. A life of righteousness creates a memory that continues to bless long after the person is gone.
verses.memory_phrase: Live so you are remembered with blessing.
verses.prayer: God, help me live in a way that blesses the people around me — and leaves behind a good memory.
verses.discussion_question: Who is someone you remember with a warm, happy feeling? What made them so special?
verses.family_discussion: What legacy are we building as a family — what will our children and grandchildren remember and be blessed by?
verses.do_it_today: Think of someone whose memory is a blessing to you. Write them a note or tell someone about them today.
verses.illustration_prompt: A child sitting with grandparents, listening to stories, warm memories glowing around the family like soft golden light, gentle watercolour scene

verses.kjv: The wise in heart will receive commandments: but a prating fool shall fall.
verses.little_bible: A person who is truly wise in their heart will gladly receive God's instructions. But the person who just keeps chattering without listening will stumble.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: A wise heart receives instruction gladly.
verses.meaning: Wisdom in the heart expresses itself in receptivity to commands and instruction. The 'prating fool' substitutes talk for obedience and ends up falling.
verses.memory_phrase: Wise hearts receive instruction.
verses.prayer: God, make my heart truly wise — ready to receive Your instructions and obey them, not just talk about them.
verses.discussion_question: What is the difference between talking a lot about something and actually doing it?
verses.family_discussion: How do we help our children move from hearing wisdom to receiving and living it — closing the gap between knowledge and obedience?
verses.do_it_today: Choose one thing you know you should do (that you have been putting off). Stop talking about it — just do it today.
verses.illustration_prompt: A child listening carefully with an open heart versus a child chattering away without listening, the listening child growing in light, watercolour comparison

verses.kjv: He that walketh uprightly walketh surely: but he that perverteth his ways shall be known.
verses.little_bible: The person who walks with honesty and integrity walks securely — they have nothing to worry about! But the person who twists and hides their ways will eventually be found out.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Walk honestly and you walk safely.
verses.meaning: Integrity provides security — the honest person has no need to fear exposure. But crooked living is inherently unstable and will eventually be revealed.
verses.memory_phrase: Walk honestly — walk safely.
verses.prayer: God, help me walk uprightly — with nothing to hide — so I can walk with confidence and peace.
verses.discussion_question: How does it feel to have nothing to hide? How does it feel when you are hiding something wrong?
verses.family_discussion: How do we build a family culture of transparency and integrity — where there is safety in honesty and no need to hide?
verses.do_it_today: Is there anything you are currently hiding? Take a small step toward honesty about it today.
verses.illustration_prompt: A child walking confidently on a clear bright path, secure and unhurried, compared to a figure walking nervously on a shadowed crooked road, watercolour style

verses.kjv: He that winketh with the eye causeth sorrow: but a prating fool shall fall.
verses.little_bible: The person who gives deceptive winks — who sends secret, dishonest signals — causes real pain. And the one who only chatters on will come to ruin.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Dishonest signals cause sorrow.
verses.meaning: Deceptive communication — secret signals, dishonest gestures — causes harm. Sorrow is the fruit of deceptive relationships.
verses.memory_phrase: Be honest in every signal you send.
verses.prayer: God, help me be honest in everything I say AND in everything I signal — no hidden tricks or deceptions.
verses.discussion_question: Have you ever felt hurt because someone was not being fully honest — even if they did not lie in words? What happened?
verses.family_discussion: How do we teach children about the different ways people can be dishonest — not just in words but in hints, omissions, and signals?
verses.do_it_today: Practice being fully open and direct with one person today — no hints, no mixed signals. Just honest words.
verses.illustration_prompt: A child giving a deceptive wink to another child who looks confused and hurt, compared to a child communicating clearly and kindly, watercolour illustration

verses.kjv: The mouth of a righteous man is a well of life: but violence covereth the mouth of the wicked.
verses.little_bible: The words of a righteous person are like a well of fresh, life-giving water — they refresh and nourish everyone! But the wicked person's words only bring harm.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Righteous words give life like a fresh well.
verses.meaning: The image of a 'well of life' is beautiful — righteous speech continually provides refreshment and nourishment to those who receive it.
verses.memory_phrase: Let your words be a well of life.
verses.prayer: God, let my words be like fresh water — refreshing and life-giving to everyone who hears them.
verses.discussion_question: Who is someone whose words feel like fresh water to you — words that encourage and refresh you? What makes their words that way?
verses.family_discussion: Are our family's words a 'well of life' — consistently refreshing and nourishing? Where can we improve?
verses.do_it_today: Say three refreshing, life-giving things to people around you today — words that encourage and build up.
verses.illustration_prompt: A child speaking kind words that flow outward as streams of clear water, reaching others and refreshing them, warm watercolour scene

verses.kjv: Hatred stirreth up strifes: but love covereth all sins.
verses.little_bible: Hate causes fights to grow bigger and bigger. But love covers over wrongs — it chooses kindness even when things go wrong!
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Love covers wrongs. Hate stirs up fights.
verses.meaning: Love does not ignore sin but chooses to handle it with grace rather than escalation. It provides a covering — protection and reconciliation rather than exposure and conflict.
verses.memory_phrase: Love covers. Hate stirs up strife.
verses.prayer: God, help me choose love when I am tempted to be unkind or stirred up in anger. Teach me to cover, not to fight.
verses.discussion_question: Have you ever seen love make a fight smaller instead of bigger? What did the loving person do?
verses.family_discussion: How do we practise 'covering' — choosing grace over conflict escalation — in our family relationships? (See also 1 Peter 4:8.)
verses.do_it_today: When a conflict arises today, ask: 'How can love cover this?' Then do that thing, even if it feels hard.
verses.illustration_prompt: Two hands reaching out to cover a small fire (representing conflict) with a warm gentle covering of love and peace, the fire going out, watercolour style

verses.kjv: In the lips of him that hath understanding wisdom is found: but a rod is for the back of him that is void of understanding.
verses.little_bible: When someone truly understands, their lips speak wisdom — it just comes out naturally. But the person who lacks understanding needs correction to learn.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Understanding makes wisdom natural in your words.
verses.meaning: True understanding produces wise speech organically. The absence of understanding requires external correction. Inner transformation is better than external enforcement.
verses.memory_phrase: Understanding makes wisdom natural.
verses.prayer: God, fill me with such deep understanding that wisdom flows naturally from what I say.
verses.discussion_question: Have you ever heard someone speak and thought: 'That was really wise'? What made it sound like wisdom?
verses.family_discussion: How do we cultivate understanding in our children — going deeper than rules to internalised wisdom that shapes speech and behaviour from the inside?
verses.do_it_today: Before speaking in any conversation today, pause for two seconds and think: 'What is the wise thing to say here?'
verses.illustration_prompt: A child speaking thoughtfully with gentle light around their words, others leaning in to listen, warmth and understanding visible on every face, watercolour style

verses.kjv: Wise men lay up knowledge: but the mouth of the foolish is near destruction.
verses.little_bible: Wise people store up knowledge — they keep learning and holding onto what they know. But the foolish person's mouth gets them into trouble!
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Wise people store knowledge. Fools talk their way into trouble.
verses.meaning: Wisdom involves active accumulation of knowledge — gathering and storing it carefully. Fools, by contrast, discharge their thoughts carelessly and it brings ruin.
verses.memory_phrase: Store knowledge. Speak carefully.
verses.prayer: God, help me store up what I learn from You — and help me speak carefully so my words do not get me into trouble.
verses.discussion_question: How do you store up knowledge? What do you do to remember important things you have learned?
verses.family_discussion: How do we help our children actively accumulate wisdom — through reading, memorisation, journaling, or discussion — rather than passively consuming content?
verses.do_it_today: Write down one wise thing you have learned this week. Put it somewhere safe so you can remember it.
verses.illustration_prompt: A child carefully placing glowing pieces of knowledge into a treasure chest, thoughtfully and deliberately, contrasted with careless words flying out of a foolish figure's mouth, watercolour illustration

verses.kjv: The rich man's wealth is his strong city: the destruction of the poor is their poverty.
verses.little_bible: Wealth can feel like a strong city — like protection. But poverty can feel like it breaks everything down. This is how life works in the world.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Wealth feels like protection. Poverty feels like a wall of trouble.
verses.meaning: This is an observation about the world as it is, not a prescription. Wealth provides security in this age; poverty creates vulnerability. Proverbs sees this clearly and honestly.
verses.memory_phrase: God is our true strong city.
verses.prayer: God, whether I have much or little, help me find my security in You — not in what I own.
verses.discussion_question: Have you ever felt very safe and protected? What gave you that feeling? Can only money do that?
verses.family_discussion: How do we help our children find their security in God rather than in wealth, status, or possessions?
verses.do_it_today: Name three things that make you feel truly safe. Is God on that list? Should He be first?
verses.illustration_prompt: A strong city on a hill gleaming in the sunlight — but an even brighter light above it representing God as the true strong tower, watercolour style

verses.kjv: The labour of the righteous tendeth to life: the fruit of the wicked to sin.
verses.little_bible: The work of a righteous person grows toward life and goodness. But everything the wicked person produces only leads to more sin.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Righteous work leads to life.
verses.meaning: The direction of our work matters as much as the work itself. Righteous labour moves toward life and flourishing; wicked labour, whatever it produces, moves toward sin.
verses.memory_phrase: Righteous work leads to life.
verses.prayer: God, help my work — every bit of it — move in the direction of life and goodness.
verses.discussion_question: Can you think of work that is good and leads to life? Can you think of work that leads to harm?
verses.family_discussion: How do we help our children think about the direction of their work and gifts — not just what they do but where it is leading?
verses.do_it_today: Look at what you are working on today. Ask: 'Does this tend toward life or away from it?' Adjust if needed.
verses.illustration_prompt: A child tending a garden that is blooming with life, their work clearly leading to beauty and growth, warm sunshine watercolour scene

verses.kjv: He is in the way of life that keepeth instruction: but he that refuseth reproof erreth.
verses.little_bible: The person who holds onto instruction is on the road to life — they are going the right way! But the one who refuses correction wanders off the path.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Keep instruction and stay on life's road.
verses.meaning: Keeping instruction is not passive — it is actively holding on. Refusing reproof is not neutral — it causes you to stray. The difference is the road you are on.
verses.memory_phrase: Keep instruction — stay on the right road.
verses.prayer: God, help me hold onto instruction tightly — not push it away — so I stay on the right road.
verses.discussion_question: What happens when you ignore the directions on a map or GPS? What happens when you ignore God's instructions?
verses.family_discussion: How do we help our children see correction not as punishment but as navigation — keeping them on the right road?
verses.do_it_today: When you receive any instruction today — at home, at school, from the Bible — actively choose to hold onto it rather than forget it.
verses.illustration_prompt: A child clutching a glowing instruction scroll while walking a bright path, contrasted with another child dropping the scroll and wandering off into a shadowy detour, watercolour style

verses.kjv: He that hideth hatred with lying lips, and he that uttereth a slander, is a fool.
verses.little_bible: Pretending to be friendly while secretly hating someone is foolish — and so is spreading bad stories about people. Both are the actions of a fool.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Hidden hatred and spreading bad stories are foolish.
verses.meaning: Hypocrisy (hiding hatred behind kind words) and slander are both forms of dishonesty about others. Both characterise the fool.
verses.memory_phrase: Honesty and kindness — not fake friendship.
verses.prayer: God, keep me from pretending to be kind while hiding bad feelings. Help me be honest and truly loving.
verses.discussion_question: Have you ever had a friend who was kind to your face but said unkind things behind your back? How did it feel?
verses.family_discussion: How do we teach our children to deal with conflict and negative feelings honestly — rather than hiding them or spreading them through gossip?
verses.do_it_today: If you have any hidden hard feelings toward someone, find a right and honest way to address them today. Pray about it first.
verses.illustration_prompt: A child with a smiling face but dark shadows hiding behind it, contrasted with a child whose face and heart match — honest and open, watercolour comparison

verses.kjv: In the multitude of words there wanteth not sin: but he that refraineth his lips is wise.
verses.little_bible: The more you talk and talk without stopping, the more likely you are to say something wrong! But the person who is careful with their words is truly wise.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Too many words lead to trouble. Careful words are wise.
verses.meaning: Excessive talk creates more opportunities for sin — carelessness, exaggeration, unkindness, and foolishness multiply with wordiness. Restraint is wisdom.
verses.memory_phrase: Fewer, careful words are wisest.
verses.prayer: God, help me speak less and think more — so that when I do speak, my words are good and wise.
verses.discussion_question: Have you ever said too much and then wished you had not? What happened?
verses.family_discussion: How do we cultivate thoughtful speech in our family — valuing quality over quantity in our words?
verses.do_it_today: Today, practice saying less. When you feel the urge to talk a lot, pause and choose the most important thing to say.
verses.illustration_prompt: A child with a gentle hand over their mouth — not in shame, but in thoughtful restraint — choosing words carefully, peaceful watercolour scene

verses.kjv: The tongue of the just is as choice silver: the heart of the wicked is little worth.
verses.little_bible: The words of a righteous person are like precious silver — valuable and beautiful. But the heart of the wicked produces nothing of real worth.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Righteous words are as precious as silver.
verses.meaning: The value of speech is tied to the heart that produces it. Righteous words are genuinely precious — like silver that has been refined and purified.
verses.memory_phrase: Righteous words are precious as silver.
verses.prayer: God, purify my heart so that what comes out of my mouth is like precious, refined silver — valuable and good.
verses.discussion_question: If your words were worth money, would they be copper, silver, or gold? What would make them more valuable?
verses.family_discussion: How do we help our children understand that the value of their words flows from the health of their hearts — and invest in heart formation?
verses.do_it_today: Say something today that is worth remembering — a wise, encouraging, or deeply true word to someone who needs it.
verses.illustration_prompt: Golden words and silver words flowing from an open mouth like precious metal, shining and valuable, warm watercolour illustration

verses.kjv: The lips of the righteous feed many: but fools die for want of wisdom.
verses.little_bible: The righteous person's words feed and nourish many people — like a good meal for their souls! But fools, having rejected wisdom, end up starved of the very thing they needed.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Righteous lips feed many people.
verses.meaning: Righteous speech has a multiplying, feeding quality — it nourishes those who receive it. By contrast, fools who reject wisdom ultimately perish for lack of it.
verses.memory_phrase: Let your words feed many.
verses.prayer: God, help my words feed and nourish the people around me. Use my lips to give others what they need.
verses.discussion_question: Who is someone whose words have truly fed you — made you stronger, wiser, or happier? What did they say?
verses.family_discussion: How do our family conversations nourish one another — building up, teaching, encouraging? Are we feeding each other well?
verses.do_it_today: Say one word today that nourishes someone — that gives them strength, encouragement, or wisdom. Mean it.
verses.illustration_prompt: A child speaking and their words flowing outward like warm bread being shared, nourishing a group of people around them, abundance and warmth, watercolour style

verses.kjv: The blessing of the LORD, it maketh rich, and he addeth no sorrow with it.
verses.little_bible: The blessing that comes from God — true blessing — makes you rich in the best way. And the wonderful thing is: His blessing brings no sadness or regret with it!
verses.little_reader_adaptation: God's blessing makes truly rich with no sorrow.
verses.meaning: Worldly wealth often comes with sorrow — anxiety, conflict, or compromise. But the wealth that comes from God's blessing is pure — it brings no such shadow with it.
verses.memory_phrase: God's blessing brings joy with no sorrow.
verses.prayer: God, bless my family with Your true blessing — the kind that brings no sorrow or regret. That is the best kind!
verses.discussion_question: Have you ever gotten something you really wanted but then felt empty or disappointed? How is God's blessing different?
verses.family_discussion: How do we recognise and celebrate the blessings of the Lord in our family — especially the blessings that come with joy and no regret attached?
verses.do_it_today: Name one blessing from God in your life that brings only happiness — no regret, no sorrow. Thank Him for it.
verses.illustration_prompt: A family receiving a warm glowing blessing from above, their faces full of pure uncomplicated joy, soft morning light watercolour scene

verses.kjv: It is as sport to a fool to do mischief: but a man of understanding hath wisdom.
verses.little_bible: A foolish person thinks causing trouble is fun — like a game! But a person of true understanding chooses wisdom instead.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Fools think mischief is fun. Wise people choose wisdom.
verses.meaning: The fool treats wrongdoing as entertainment — a moral failure that reveals deep disorder. The person of understanding has wisdom as their 'sport' — their delight.
verses.memory_phrase: Wise people delight in wisdom, not mischief.
verses.prayer: God, help me find delight in wisdom and good things — not in causing trouble or mischief.
verses.discussion_question: Why do you think some people find trouble-making fun? What can be even more fun that is also good?
verses.family_discussion: How do we provide our children with genuinely exciting and joyful activities that do not involve mischief or harm?
verses.do_it_today: Find something truly fun that is also good and wise to do today. Enjoy it fully!
verses.illustration_prompt: A child laughing while making mischief versus a child laughing with genuine delight over something wise and good, watercolour comparison

verses.kjv: The fear of the wicked, it shall come upon him: but the desire of the righteous shall be granted.
verses.little_bible: The very things the wicked person dreads will come to them. But the righteous person's deepest wishes and desires — God will grant them!
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Righteous desires are granted by God.
verses.meaning: There is an ironic justice in life: the wicked get their feared outcome; the righteous get their desired outcome. God's order ultimately prevails.
verses.memory_phrase: Righteous desires — God will grant them.
verses.prayer: God, my desire is to know You and do good. Please grant the desires of my heart, as You promise.
verses.discussion_question: What is the deepest thing you want in your life — not just a toy or treat, but something really important? Can you trust God with that desire?
verses.family_discussion: How do we help our children cultivate righteous desires — wanting what God wants — rather than just wanting what the world offers?
verses.do_it_today: Write down one deep, righteous desire for your life. Pray it to God: 'God, this is what I desire. I trust You with it.'
verses.illustration_prompt: A child holding out open hands with a glowing desire in them, God's light reaching down to receive and bless it, peaceful and trusting watercolour scene

verses.kjv: As the whirlwind passeth, so is the wicked no more: but the righteous is an everlasting foundation.
verses.little_bible: The wicked person is swept away like a storm that comes and goes in an instant. But the righteous person is like an everlasting foundation — solid, lasting, and sure!
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Righteous people are an everlasting foundation.
verses.meaning: Wickedness is ultimately temporary — it passes like a storm. Righteousness endures — it is foundational and lasting, unshaken by the storms that sweep everything else away.
verses.memory_phrase: Righteousness is an everlasting foundation.
verses.prayer: God, help me build my life on the everlasting foundation of righteousness — so nothing can sweep me away.
verses.discussion_question: What is the most solid and immovable thing you have ever seen or touched? How is righteousness even more solid?
verses.family_discussion: What is our family built on — what is our foundation? How do we ensure it is righteousness and God's Word rather than temporary things?
verses.do_it_today: Put your feet flat on the floor and feel how solid it is. Say: 'Righteousness is even more solid than this. I will stand on it.'
verses.illustration_prompt: A strong house on a rock foundation as a storm swirls around it — the foundation gleaming with righteousness, steady and immovable, beautiful watercolour scene

verses.kjv: As vinegar to the teeth, and as smoke to the eyes, so is the sluggard to them that send him.
verses.little_bible: A lazy person sent on an errand is like vinegar on your teeth or smoke in your eyes — unpleasant, irritating, and not at all helpful!
verses.little_reader_adaptation: A lazy worker is as unpleasant as smoke in your eyes.
verses.meaning: The sluggard is described through two vivid, uncomfortable sensory images. Laziness is not just personally harmful — it is genuinely irritating and unreliable to those who depend on you.
verses.memory_phrase: Be reliable — not like smoke in the eyes.
verses.prayer: God, help me be the kind of person people can count on — reliable, diligent, and worth trusting.
verses.discussion_question: Has someone ever let you down when you were counting on them? How did it feel? How do you want others to feel about you?
verses.family_discussion: How do we cultivate reliability and follow-through in our children — the habit of doing what they said they would do?
verses.do_it_today: Is there something someone is counting on you to do? Do it today — completely and on time.
verses.illustration_prompt: Two vivid panels: vinegar making a face scrunch up; smoke making eyes water — and a lazy figure being sent on an errand, watercolour humorous illustration

verses.kjv: The fear of the LORD prolongeth days: but the years of the wicked shall be shortened.
verses.little_bible: Loving and respecting God adds more days to your life! But the wicked person's years are cut short.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Fearing God makes your life longer and richer.
verses.meaning: The fear of the Lord — expressed in righteous living — generally leads to longer, better life. This is a wisdom principle, not an absolute promise: godly choices tend to be healthier choices.
verses.memory_phrase: Fear God and your days are lengthened.
verses.prayer: God, I want a long, full life that honours You. Help me walk in Your fear and love every day.
verses.discussion_question: What are some choices that help you live a longer, better life? Can you see how many of them are also wise choices?
verses.family_discussion: How do we connect health, longevity, and wellbeing to wisdom and the fear of the Lord — rather than just to diet or exercise?
verses.do_it_today: Make one wise, healthy choice today. It might be sleep, food, exercise, or choosing peace over anger.
verses.illustration_prompt: A long vibrant path stretching into a beautiful golden sunset, a figure walking it with energy and joy, representing a long and God-honouring life, watercolour style

verses.kjv: The hope of the righteous shall be gladness: but the expectation of the wicked shall perish.
verses.little_bible: The hopes of the righteous person will come true and bring gladness! But the wicked person's expectations will melt away.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Righteous hope leads to gladness.
verses.meaning: Hope is not merely optimism — the righteous person's hope is rooted in God and will be fulfilled in gladness. The wicked person's expectation, not rooted in truth, will perish.
verses.memory_phrase: Righteous hope leads to joy.
verses.prayer: God, I put my hope in You. I know it will lead to gladness — because You keep Your promises.
verses.discussion_question: What is something you are really hoping for? Is it a hope you can trust God with?
verses.family_discussion: How do we cultivate righteous, God-centred hope in our children — expectation based on God's character and promises rather than wishful thinking?
verses.do_it_today: Write one hope you are trusting God with. Put it in an envelope. Seal it with the words: 'God, I trust You with this.'
verses.illustration_prompt: A child looking forward with bright hopeful eyes toward a glowing horizon of gladness, peace and anticipation in their expression, morning light watercolour scene

verses.kjv: The way of the LORD is strength to the upright: but destruction shall be to the workers of iniquity.
verses.little_bible: God's way is like a source of strength for the person who walks uprightly — it makes them strong! But for the person doing wrong, the same road is their undoing.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: God's way gives strength to those who walk uprightly.
verses.meaning: The same road — God's way — has opposite effects depending on who walks it. For the upright it provides strength and security; for the wicked it becomes a path of destruction.
verses.memory_phrase: God's way is strength for the upright.
verses.prayer: God, let Your way be my strength today. I choose to walk in it — not away from it.
verses.discussion_question: Have you ever felt strong because you made a right and brave choice? That is what this verse is talking about.
verses.family_discussion: How do we communicate to our children that obedience to God is not weakness but a source of genuine strength and courage?
verses.do_it_today: Do something today that is right and might feel hard. Notice the strength that comes from choosing God's way.
verses.illustration_prompt: A child walking upright on a glowing strong road, energy and confidence in every step, contrasted with a shadow figure stumbling on the same road, watercolour style

verses.kjv: The righteous shall never be removed: but the wicked shall not inhabit the earth.
verses.little_bible: The righteous person is permanently established — they will never be uprooted! But the wicked will not have a lasting home in the earth.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Righteous people are permanently established.
verses.meaning: Righteousness provides permanence and rootedness — the righteous person is not swept away. This echoes Psalm 1 and the image of the tree planted by water.
verses.memory_phrase: The righteous are never removed.
verses.prayer: God, root me in righteousness so deep that nothing in life can uproot me from You.
verses.discussion_question: Have you ever seen a tree with very deep roots? What can it survive? How is a righteous person like that tree?
verses.family_discussion: How do we as a family put down deep roots — in Scripture, prayer, community, and character — so we are not easily moved?
verses.do_it_today: Find a tree outside. Look at how firmly it is rooted. Say: 'I want to be that rooted in righteousness.'
verses.illustration_prompt: A great tree with deep visible roots, firmly planted in beautiful earth, glowing with life and permanence, small child looking up at it in awe, watercolour style

verses.kjv: The mouth of the just bringeth forth wisdom: but the froward tongue shall be cut out.
verses.little_bible: The mouth of the just person brings forth wisdom — it grows and produces like a good fruit tree! But the twisted, dishonest tongue leads only to being silenced.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Just mouths grow wisdom. Twisted tongues are silenced.
verses.meaning: The just person's mouth is generative — it produces wisdom the way a tree produces fruit. The 'froward' (perverse, twisted) tongue ultimately silences itself through its own destructiveness.
verses.memory_phrase: Just mouths bring forth wisdom.
verses.prayer: God, make my mouth like a fruit tree — growing and bringing forth wisdom for everyone around me.
verses.discussion_question: What does it mean to 'bring forth' wisdom? How does wisdom grow from a person who keeps choosing what is right?
verses.family_discussion: How do we grow wisdom in our speech — not just avoiding wrong words but actively cultivating the good fruit of wise words?
verses.do_it_today: Say one wise thing today that you have been holding back — something true and good that someone needs to hear.
verses.illustration_prompt: Words growing from a child's mouth like fruit from a tree — ripe, golden, good — abundant and giving life to those nearby, watercolour style

verses.kjv: The lips of the righteous know what is acceptable: but the mouth of the wicked speaketh frowardness.
verses.little_bible: The righteous person's lips know what is good and right to say — they choose words that are acceptable and kind. But the wicked person speaks twisted, harmful things.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Righteous lips know what is good to say.
verses.meaning: Righteous speech is characterised by knowing what is 'acceptable' — what is fitting, appropriate, and pleasing. This is wisdom's discernment applied to communication.
verses.memory_phrase: Know what is right to say — and say it.
verses.prayer: God, give my lips the wisdom to know what is acceptable and good to say — and the courage to say it.
verses.discussion_question: How can you know if what you are about to say is acceptable and good? What tests can you use before you speak?
verses.family_discussion: How do we help our children develop the discernment to know what is appropriate to say in different contexts — truth-telling with love, speaking well of others, choosing the right moment?
verses.do_it_today: Before every major conversation today, ask: 'What is the acceptable, right thing to say here?' Then say it.
verses.illustration_prompt: A child pausing thoughtfully before speaking, a warm glowing filter of wisdom around their words, choosing carefully and then speaking with a smile, watercolour style
